message:
feat(abi): .init_array bootstrap + raise/kill/sigaction executors

crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/lib.rs:

- Linker-invoked process-init hook. A #[used] extern "C" fn
  pointer is placed into the .init_array ELF section (gated to
  (not(test), target_os = \"linux\")), which the dynamic linker
  walks before main(). The function calls
  io_internal_abi::bootstrap_host_libio_exports() so the host
  glibc _IO_list_all chain is patched before any user code can
  allocate a FILE * against it. Prevents a class of exit-time
  crashes where the atexit flush would see un-patched host
  stdio symbols once FrankenLibC had already taken over stdio
  exports.

crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:

- execute_raise_case: reads sig from inputs, calls
  frankenlibc_abi::signal_abi::raise, records the numeric result.
- execute_kill_case: reads pid + sig, calls signal_abi::kill,
  records the numeric result.
- execute_sigaction_case: reads signum, zeroes a libc::sigaction
  on the stack, calls signal_abi::sigaction, records the numeric
  result. Matches the dispatch arms that the previous commit
  added to execute_fixture_case.
